codeReview.summary: The feature adds activation/deactivation APIs, typed errors, and extensive tests, but activateSkill does not actually verify that an existing symlink points to the requested repo path before treating activation as an idempotent success.

issues.description: activateSkill decides "already active" by calling checkSymlink(), and that helper only checks whether activeDir/skillName is some non-broken symlink (live.ts:695-715). If the symlink points to the wrong repo or stale location, activateSkill returns alreadyInState=true and leaves the incorrect target in place instead of repointing it to repoPath/skillName, so VAL-SKILL-005/expectedBehavior is not satisfied. The activation tests cover only generic active/inactive states and do not exercise a wrong-target symlink, which is why this regression slipped through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill explicitly requires TDD (write tests first and run the package tests red before implementation), but this worker implemented types/errors/live code before adding activation tests. The handoff still reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true, so the recorded skill-feedback signal is unreliable for this run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend-worker/SKILL.md:28-56 requires tests-before-implementation and an initial failing `npm test -- --filter <package>` run. In the transcript skeleton for worker session 6e27ee19-c4f8-4ad2-85e5-8af1dac91320, edits to packages/skill-ops/src/types.ts, src/errors.ts, src/live.ts, and src/index.ts happen before the first edit to packages/skill-ops/test/skill-ops.test.ts and before the first `npm test -- --filter @codex-fleet/skill-ops` run; the handoff JSON nevertheless sets skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Review failed. activateSkill currently treats any valid symlink as already active, even when it points to the wrong target, so the feature does not guarantee the active link points at the requested skill repo path. I also noted a shared-state mismatch: the worker did not follow the backend-worker TDD procedure even though the handoff marked the procedure as followed.
